title: Brevo vs Klaviyo (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Brevo and Klaviyo: pricing, features, and best use cases for email marketing and automation.
h1: Brevo vs Klaviyo: Complete Comparison Guide
intro: Both Brevo and Klaviyo are established email marketing platforms founded in 2012, but they serve different business needs. Brevo excels as an all-in-one CRM with multi-channel capabilities, while Klaviyo specializes in e-commerce automation. Understanding their key differences will help you choose the right tool for your marketing strategy.
choose_a_if: You need an affordable freemium plan with 100,000 subscribers included
You want multi-channel marketing including WhatsApp, SMS, and live chat
You require a built-in CRM and transactional email API capabilities
You're looking to monetize through affiliate commissions (5 EUR per free signup + 100 EUR per paid)
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce store on Shopify or WooCommerce with specialized needs
You need advanced predictive analytics and behavioral segmentation for product recommendations
You want deep integration with review management and customer feedback tools
You prioritize platform maturity with strong G2 ratings (4.6/5) for e-commerce workflows

faq.a: It depends on your needs. Brevo is better for businesses wanting an affordable all-in-one platform with multi-channel capabilities and a generous free tier. Klaviyo is superior for e-commerce brands needing specialized features like predictive analytics and deep Shopify integration. Both have strong ratings, so the choice should align with your specific use case.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Brevo or Klaviyo?
faq.a: Brevo is significantly cheaper overall. Its free tier supports up to 100,000 subscribers with no limitations, while Klaviyo's free tier caps at 500 subscribers. Paid plans start at $25/month for Brevo versus $45/month for Klaviyo, making Brevo the more budget-friendly option for most businesses.

faq.q: Can I switch from Klaviyo to Brevo?
faq.a: Yes, you can migrate from Klaviyo to Brevo. Both platforms support standard data export formats and integrations. Brevo offers customer support to help with migrations, though you should plan the transition carefully to maintain your email list segmentation and automation workflows during the switch.
